mission.title: kubevela: [Feature] Support for explicit versioning of definitions
mission.description: [Feature] Support for explicit versioning of definitions. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevela deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation.

steps.title: Review kubevela configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
This feature request is to add the facility for component/trait etc definitions to explicitly specify a version as part of the definition.

The Application will then have the ability to refer to the definition version when using a definition.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Support for explicit versioning of definitions
steps.description:
I like the proposal, but have some doubts on the options that are being proposed. In terms of calling component versions I think adding the version as proposed by  will provide a user friendly approach for users deploying applications. To add an example of what I am understanding that would
```yaml
# latest (no version specified)
    type: wb-service
```

steps.title: Upgrade kubevela to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kubevela:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubevela kubevela/kubevela --namespace kubevela
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela
helm list -n kubevela
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Support for explicit versioning of definitions" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I like the proposal, but have some doubts on the options that are being proposed. In terms of calling component versions I think adding the version as proposed by  will provide a user friendly approach for users deploying applications.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible.
